channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.124
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.124 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Regional Endpoints for Azure Container Registry Geo-Replication — Now in Public Preview
item.description: By Johnson Shi, Zoey (Zhuyu) Li, Huangli Wu What's new Regional endpoints for geo-replicated Azure Container Registries are now in public preview. See the feature's official MS Learn documentation. If you've been following since the private preview announcement, here's what changed: No feature flag...
item.dc:creator: johnsonshi_msft

item.title: Preview of multiparty analytics with Azure Confidential Clean Rooms
item.description: Today, we are excited to announce the preview of multiparty analytics feature of Azure Confidential Clean Rooms, a fully managed service that allows customers and their partners to securely analyze privacy-sensitive datasets from multiple parties. It uses confidential compute enabled Apache...
item.dc:creator: Deepak_JV

item.title: Azure Speech at Build 2026: Powering Voice Agents with Real-Time and Life-like Experiences
item.description: Voice is rapidly becoming the default interface for AI. At Build 2026, Azure Speech is making it dramatically easier to ship production-grade voice experiences that feel real, responsive, and global. From agentic, low-latency Voice Live experiences built natively into Foundry Agent Service, to a...
item.dc:creator: DONGLI

item.title: Azure Monitor Health Model (Preview): What's New!
item.description: Azure Monitor Health Model is a modern observability capability that brings together telemetry, architecture, and business context of your workloads to generate health insights. It continuously aggregates signals across dependencies, producing a single, actionable health state which reduces alert...
item.dc:creator: shijain13

item.title: Pod CIDR Expansion Generally Available and IP Address Planning on Azure CNI Overlay
item.description: In networking with Azure CNI Overlay, the cluster-wide pod CIDR is logically partitioned into smaller “node” blocks where each node is assigned a fixed CIDR slice (/24) by Azure. This decouples pod networking from the VNet address space entirely because pods receive addresses from a private CIDR...
item.dc:creator: Sam_Foo

item.title: Release 2026-05-29
item.description: Release Notes - 2026-05-29 Monitor the release status by regions at AKS-Release-Tracker. Vulnerabilities addressed by AKS releases can be tracked at CVE API viewer. Announcements of upcoming changes and retirements Revision asm-1-27 of the Istio-based service mesh add-on has been deprecated. Please...
item.dc:creator: dyu1208

item.title: Frameworks only matter when they force decisions
item.description: Frameworks mean nothing, until they change what gets built! In this article we discuss how Git-Ape turns architecture and governance into delivery controls on Azure because, if frameworks do not shape delivery decisions, they are just decoration. Cloud teams do not have a framework problem. They...
item.dc:creator: davidwright, Arnaud Lheureux, Suzanne Daniels

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Voice sync for avatar support in Voice Live API in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: In public preview, Microsoft Foundry now supports avatar voice sync with custom voices in the Voice Live API, allowing developers to pair branded or persona-specific text-to-speech voices with real-time avatar experiences. This update helps teams building
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Files assessments are now available worldwide using Azure Migrate
item.description: We’re pleased to announce that Azure Migrate now generally supports discovery and assessment of SMB and NFS file shares hosted on Windows and Linux servers. This gives customers a complete, data-driven view of their file share environment and a clear path
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Hosted MCP Servers in Connector Namespace (Preview)
item.description: Imagine you've built an agent and you want to give it access to tools via MCP servers. Local servers won't work because your agent can't connect to them in production. Wouldn't it be great if you could quickly stand up secure, enterprise-ready remote MCP servers that your agent can use? This is...
item.dc:creator: lily-ma

item.title: Fix with Copilot for failing Actions now in Pro, Pro+, and Max
item.description: When a GitHub Actions job fails, Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max subscribers can now ask Copilot cloud agent to fix it in one click. Click the Fix with Copilot button… The post Fix with Copilot for failing Actions now in Pro, Pro+, and Max appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Agent tasks REST API now available for Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max
item.description: Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max users can now programmatically start and track Copilot cloud agent tasks with the Agent tasks REST API, available in public preview. Copilot cloud agent works… The post Agent tasks REST API now available for Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: AI pipelines in Azure HorizonDB
item.description: AI pipelines for Azure HorizonDB is now available in public preview. AI pipelines let you describe an AI data ingestion workflow, chunking, embedding, extraction, generation, and ranking, declaratively in SQL, and run it as a fault-tolerant pipeline insid
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Ingest OTLP signals into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description: Azure Monitor's support for native ingestion of OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) signals is now generally available. This enables you to send telemetry data directly from OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications and platforms to Azure Monitor. You can configu
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Introducing the New Browser Automation Tool with Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description: AI agents can reason through complex problems—but they hit a wall when workflows require clicking buttons, filling forms, or navigating web interfaces. The Browser Automation Tool bridges that gap, giving your agents the ability to interact with any website the same way a human would. Here's what's...
item.dc:creator: NandiniMuralidharan

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Tool search in Microsoft Foundry toolboxes
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds tool search to Foundry toolboxes in public preview, giving developers and admins a fast way to find the right tool inside large, multi-team catalogs. As your toolbox grows, sending every tool definition on every turn gets expensive.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: APIM Support for Foundry Models in Azure AI Search
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings Azure API Management support to all Foundry model integrations in Azure AI Search, now in public preview. Enterprise platform engineers and AI solution teams running large-scale RAG pipelines can place Foundry and Azure OpenAI mod
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL as a knowledge source in Foundry IQ
item.description: Microsoft Foundry adds Azure SQL Database as a first-class Knowledge Source in Azure AI Search, available in public preview. Enterprise developers and solution architects building Copilot, RAG, and agentic experiences can now expose authoritative tables a
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Discovery
item.description: Microsoft Discovery is now generally available, providing research and development organizations an enterprise platform for building and governing agentic AI workflows across scientific and engineering disciplines. Also available in preview is the Microso
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Private Connectivity for Azure AI Search and Foundry Knowledge Bases
item.description: Azure AI Search and Foundry Knowledge Bases now support private, end-to-end network connectivity between search resources and Foundry services. Customers can route ingestion, enrichment, retrieval, and agent traffic over Shared Private Link or Network Sec
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Fabric IQ Ontology Knowledge Source in Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description: Microsoft Foundry IQ now treats a Microsoft Fabric Ontology as a federated knowledge source in public preview, giving agents a way to query the semantic layer customers already curate in Fabric. Foundry IQ translates natural language questions into ontolo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description: Microsoft Foundry IQ reaches general availability, giving developers a managed knowledge layer that grounds agents in enterprise data without rebuilding retrieval pipelines for each project. Teams connect SharePoint, OneLake, Azure Blob, and other sources
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us
item.description: A surge in real-world attacks against agentic AI systems is reshaping how we think about risk. Based on 12 months of red teaming, this update introduces seven new failure modes, from supply chain compromise to goal hijacking, and the practical mitigations teams need now. The post Updating the...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft AI Red Team

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Content Understanding chunking and image verbalization in Azure AI Search
item.description: Azure AI Search now supports Content Understanding chunking and image verbalization in public preview, expanding the built-in skill that parses rich documents during indexing. Indexers can split documents into semantically meaningful chunks and generate t
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Larger context windows and configurable reasoning levels for GitHub Copilot
item.description: GitHub Copilot now supports larger context windows and configurable reasoning levels to help you tackle deeper, more complex work. Leverage one-million-token context windows A one-million-token context window means users can… The post Larger context windows and configurable reasoning levels for...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Customer Newsletter
item.description: What's new in Defender for Cloud? Defender for Cloud is now integrated into the Defender portal to bring together cloud security posture management and threat protection in a single experience. Read more about it here. Cloud security reporting in the Defender portal is now in public preview...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee

item.title: Giving Developers Claude Code with Azure API Management and Claude Models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Summary You want to give your engineering org Claude Code without handing out Anthropic API keys, without per-developer billing sprawl, and without losing visibility into who is spending what. This post shows a battle-tested pattern: Claude models run in Microsoft Foundry, billed through your Azure...
item.dc:creator: MuraliKumanduri

item.title: Aspire Multi-repo Rollout at Scale with Agentic AI
item.description: This is part 2 blog of the windows 365 integration journey with Aspire. This blog focus to show how Windows 365 scaled Aspire adoption with reliability patterns and an agentic AI rollout system across 50+ repos. The post Aspire Multi-repo Rollout at Scale with Agentic AI appeared first on Aspire...
item.dc:creator: Jeff Liu, Chuanbo Zhang

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor Service Level Indicators (SLI)
item.description: Azure Monitor now includes Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs), giving teams a clearer way to measure how customers actually experience their applications. Instead of relying only on infrastructure signals like CPU or isola
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era
item.description: GitHub Universe is back: returning to the historic Fort Mason Center in San Francisco on October 28–29, 2026. The post GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — May update
item.description: May 2026 brought sharper tooling for planning, reviewing, and managing your work with GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio 2026. Highlights Here’s what’s new for all GitHub Copilot users in Visual… The post GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — May update appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Enterprise Teams is now generally available
item.description: Enterprise Teams is now generally available on GitHub Enterprise Cloud. First announced in public preview last September, enterprise teams let enterprise admins define a group of users once at the… The post Enterprise Teams is now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot Chat brings richer context to pull requests
item.description: GitHub Copilot Chat now provides richer context and new capabilities when you’re working with diffs and pull requests on github.com. This feature was previously in public preview and is now… The post Copilot Chat brings richer context to pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: New Capabilities to Observe Agents in Azure Monitor
item.description: Over the last six months, we have been listening to you and building new capabilities to help you observe your agents. You’ve been sharing with us that quality issues are tricky and evaluation is critical, that agent reasoning needs to be understood, that humans must be in the loop to review select...
item.dc:creator: MattMc

item.title: Harness-Driven Agents: Secure Podcast Pipeline in Hyperlight MicroVM Sandbox
item.description: The moment the agent reached for rm -rf For most of 2024 and 2025, "agents" were a demo word. By 2026 they are something you run — autonomously, in a loop, executing code they wrote themselves a second ago. I was watching one work late one night. I had given it a goal, a handful of tools, and the...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: Accelerate Edge AI Development with Foundry Local
item.description: Why edge AI development is still hard AI is no longer confined to cloud experiments. Developers are increasingly expected to deliver AI inside apps, devices, and edge systems where responsiveness, privacy, resilience, and local control are essential. But building those experiences for production is...
item.dc:creator: samkemp, Emma Ning, Inbal Sagiv

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: May 2026
item.description: Welcome to the May edition of What's new in Microsoft Sentinel. This month’s updates focus on unified role-based access control (RBAC), ecosystem breadth, AI-agent security, and high-assurance identity. RBAC and row-level scoping are now generally available, giving security teams a single, granular...
item.dc:creator: Sowmys

item.title: Foundry Toolkit for VS Code at //build: Hosted Agents End-to-End, a Smarter Toolbox, and More
item.description: We’re excited to share what’s new for Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code at //build 2026. Since going generally available, the toolkit has kept moving fast, and this release is a big one. The headline: a complete, end-to-end Hosted Agent experience, scaffold, run, deploy, and observe without...
item.dc:creator: leoyao

item.title: How we Decide Between Keyword and Hybrid Search: 5 Enterprise Evaluation Criteria
item.description: A data-driven framework we use in enterprise deployments to decide between vector-only keyword and hybrid search, based on five measurable evaluation criteria. The post How we Decide Between Keyword and Hybrid Search: 5 Enterprise Evaluation Criteria appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Beijie Zhang

item.title: Foundry IQ: Improve recall by up to 54% with knowledge bases
item.description: By Abhishree Shetty, Alec Berntson, Alina Stoica Beck, Amaia Salvador Aguilera, Arnau Quindós Sánchez, Jieyu Cheng, Jim Singh, Jing Zhu, Lihang Li, Matt Gotteiner, Mike Kim, Thibault Gisselbrecht Agents are only valuable for the enterprise when they have access to your organization's knowledge. A...
item.dc:creator: MattGotteiner

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: MAI-Voice-2 in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry now offers MAI-Voice-2 in public preview, a first-party voice model from the Microsoft AI team. MAI-Voice-2 generates natural speech across more than 10 languages and supports voice cloning from a short reference sample, plus voice promp
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
item.description: 9 min read · June 3, 2026 · Sebastian Kohlmeier Shipping an AI agent is the easy part. Keeping it accurate, safe, and accountable in production is where teams get stuck. Agents are non-deterministic. Their behavior shifts as models update, tools change, and traffic patterns evolve and most of that...
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Kohlmeier

item.title: [Launched] General Availability: Backup your ledger files for audit with Azure confidential ledger’s new tool
item.description: Ever wondered how to allow auditing personas (i.e. security analyst in your organization or security assessment contractor) the ability to view your ledger files and establish trust in the cryptographic proofs themselves? With the new localized, web ledg
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Text Analytics for Health NextGen Playground in Azure AI Language
item.description: Azure AI Language brings the next-generation Text Analytics for Health (TA4H) playground to general availability in the Microsoft Foundry portal. Clinical informatics teams and ISVs can paste discharge summaries, doctor's notes, or research abstracts and
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: User feedback logging in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: This feature introduces end user feedback logging semantics that enables developers to capture structured feedback signals from real users interacting with AI agents—such as thumbs up/down, ratings, or custom annotations—and automatically associates that
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Self-serve custom photo avatar creation in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings self-serve custom photo avatar creation to GA in the Foundry NextGen fine-tuning portal. Enterprise customers building voice agents with avatars or producing content with avatar and speech often need a branded avatar instead of a
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GA: DCasv6 and ECasv6 confidential VMs based on 4th Generation AMD EPYC™ processors
item.description: Today, Azure has expanded its confidential computing offerings with the general availability of the DCasv6 and ECasv6 confidential VMs. Regional availability June 03 2026: Australia Central, Australia Central 2, Australia Southeast, Austria East, Belgium Central, Brazil South, Brazil Southeast,...
item.dc:creator: Rakeshginjupalli

item.title: Build Enterprise-Ready Agents with Microsoft IQ and Oracle AI Database@Azure — now with Oracle MCP
item.description: We’re making it easier to bring Oracle data into intelligent applications with Microsoft IQ and Oracle AI Database@Azure. Oracle Managed Database MCP (Model Context Protocol) remote servers are now available in Microsoft Foundry—introducing a fully managed option that simplifies AI integration...
item.dc:creator: RamKakani

item.title: DevOps for Microsoft Hosted Agents: From Terraform Apply to Production-Grade Agent Delivery
item.description: A companion piece to Infrastructure as Code for AI: Building and Deploying Microsoft Hosted Agents with Terraform. Just announced — source-code deploy (preview). Foundry has just added a second Hosted Agent deploy path alongside the container path this post covers. Instead of a container image, you...
item.dc:creator: LZhang
